title: Burns — Thermal/Chemical
immediate_action: Cool the burn under cool running water for at least 20 minutes.

steps.instruction: Cool the burn under cool (not cold/ice) running water for 20 minutes. Start immediately.
steps.tip: 20 minutes is the minimum. This is the single most important step. Lukewarm is fine.

steps.instruction: Remove clothing and jewelry near the burn UNLESS stuck to the skin.
steps.tip: Clothing retains heat. Remove rings/watches before swelling makes it impossible.

steps.instruction: Cover the burn loosely with cling film or a clean, non-fluffy material.
steps.tip: Cling film laid over (not wrapped around) is ideal. It's sterile, transparent, and doesn't stick.

steps.instruction: For chemical burns: brush off dry chemicals first, then flush with water for 20+ minutes.
steps.tip: Check the chemical's MSDS if possible. Some chemicals react with water.

steps.instruction: Call emergency services for: burns larger than the person's hand, burns on face/hands/feet/genitals, deep burns (white/brown, no pain), chemical/electrical burns, or burns in children.
steps.tip: If in doubt, always seek emergency care.

when_to_stop: Burn has been cooled for 20 minutes
do_not: Do NOT apply ice, butter, toothpaste, or cream
Do NOT remove clothing stuck to the burn
Do NOT use fluffy cotton or adhesive bandages
